# Conventions — my-saas-app

## Project Overview

A SaaS web application with a React/TypeScript frontend and Node.js/Express REST API backend. Users can manage projects, invite team members, and track tasks. Deployed on AWS with PostgreSQL via RDS and Redis for session caching.

## Tech Stack

- TypeScript 5.x (language)
- React ^18 + Next.js ^14 (frontend)
- Node.js ^20 + Express ^4 (API backend)
- PostgreSQL ^15 + Prisma ^5 ORM
- Redis ^7 (cache)
- Jest (unit/integration tests), Playwright (E2E)
- ESLint + Prettier, Tailwind CSS

## Directory Layout

- `src/app/` — Next.js App Router pages and layouts
- `src/components/` — Reusable React components
- `src/server/` — Express API routes, services, repositories
- `prisma/` — Schema and migration files
- `tests/` — Jest unit/integration tests

## Build & Dev Commands

```sh
npm ci           # install
npm run dev      # development server
npm test         # run test suite
npm run lint     # lint
npm run build    # production build
```

---

## Code Style

1. TypeScript strict mode is enabled. Never use `any` type without an explicit comment.
2. Never use `console.log/debug/warn` in production code. Use `src/lib/logger.ts`.
3. React components: PascalCase filenames and component names, named exports (not default exports).

---

## Architecture

1. Next.js App Router components are Server Components by default. Only add `'use client'` when the component needs browser APIs, state, or event handlers.

---

## Testing

1. Unit test files must be colocated: `UserCard.tsx` → `UserCard.test.tsx`. Integration tests in `tests/`.

---

## Security

1. Never hardcode secrets, API keys, or passwords. Always use environment variables.
2. All API route handlers must validate input with Zod. Never trust `req.body` without validation. (applies to: src/server/**/*.ts)
3. Never construct raw SQL with string interpolation. Use Prisma query builder or `Prisma.sql` tag. (applies to: src/server/repositories/**/*.ts)

# CROSS-SKILLS: DEGRADED — permissions.deny converted to convention text
4. Never run: `rm -rf *`
5. Never run: `curl * | bash *`
6. Never run: `sudo *` commands

---

## Git Conventions

1. Commit messages must follow Conventional Commits: `type(scope): description`. Types: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, ci.

---

## Agent Behavior

1. When reviewing PRs, flag PRs with more than 400 changed lines as too large.

---

## Frontend Specialist Role

# CROSS-SKILLS: DEGRADED — Frontend Specialist sub-agent flattened to convention section
When working in `src/app/**` or `src/components/**`:
- Follow component conventions: PascalCase files, named exports, Tailwind for styling
- Prefer Server Components; add `'use client'` only when needed
- Validate accessibility for all new UI components

---

## API Specialist Role

# CROSS-SKILLS: DEGRADED — API Specialist sub-agent flattened to convention section
When working in `src/server/**` or `prisma/**`:
- Always validate input with Zod in route handlers
- Use services for business logic, repositories for DB access
- Never expose internal errors to API clients
- Follow REST conventions for status codes
